title: Daucus azoricus
intro: Daucus azoricus, also known as Azores carrot or Carrot of the Azores, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Daucus azoricus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Daucus azoricus is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Daucus azoricus is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family comprises a large number of plant species, many of which are widely cultivated for their edible fruits, seeds, and leaves. The genus Daucus itself is a small group of flowering plants, and Daucus azoricus is one of the species within this genus. Daucus azoricus is native to the Azores, a group of islands in the Atlantic Ocean. It is a unique and endemic species to the region, found only in the wild.
